articles.title: NASA launched an emergency mission to stop the Swift Observatory from crashing to Earth
articles.summary: Engineers from Katalyst Space Technologies testing Link. | Image: NASA/Scott Wiessinger The Swift Observatory was launched in 2004, but recent solar storms have pushed its orbit lower, and it's in danger of burning up in Earth's atmosphere as soon as this year. articles.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
articles.summary: As part of an ongoing legal dispute with three Hollywood studios, Midjourney is seeking to compel those studios to reveal how they use AI themselves.

articles.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
articles.summary: Alibaba has reportedly classified Claude Code as high-risk software.

articles.title: White House deletes thousands of web pages about energy conservation as heatwave slams US
articles.summary: The US Department of Energy reportedly deleted about 6,000 pages related to energy conservation as a historic heatwave tears across the country. articles.title: Matic’s robot vacuum is getting a $250 price hike in September
articles.summary: The Matic is our favorite robot vacuum by a pretty comfortable margin. If you’ve been thinking about buying one, you may want to plan on doing it sooner than later. articles.title: Submit Your Questions: Inside The World of Online Romance Scams
articles.summary: The Yahoo Boys author Carlos Barragán will join Kate Knibbs to answer your questions about Nigeria's romance scammers.

articles.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
articles.summary: Mistral AI, which offers some open source AI models, has raised significant funding since its creation in 2023, with the ambition to “put frontier AI in the hands of everyone.”

articles.title: The square-ish phone that I wanted to love
articles.summary: A great concept that falls flat. The Ikko MindOne Pro is delightfully small. I keep calling it a square phone, which isn't quite right; the screen is square, but the phone itself is slightly rectangular. articles.title: NASA’s Hubble Spies Stellar Sparkler for July 4th
articles.summary: Red, white, and blue stars glitter like a sparkler being waved on a dark night in this new image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope.

articles.title: Where NASA Posts Its Best Space Photos, and How to Find Them

articles.title: Food Preservatives May Increase the Risk of High Blood Pressure and Cardiovascular Disease
articles.summary: Aa large-scale study demonstrates that preservatives widely used in everyday processed foods may exacerbate common health risks.

articles.title: Scientists Have Identified a New Fossil Species of Axolotl in Mexico
articles.summary: Ambystoma quetzalcoatli is the first fossil salamander to be formally identified in Mexico, revealing that axolotls have inhabited the country for millions of years.

articles.title: The only AI glossary you’ll need this year
articles.summary: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

articles.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
articles.summary: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

articles.title: The Dune keypad device can be your meeting controller and more
articles.summary: The gadget has three buttons, and it changes context based on what app you are looking at. For instance, in meeting apps and sites, it could be toggle mic, toggle video, and bring window to the front.

articles.title: 3 Nuclear Startups Hit a Big Milestone. Why It Matters—and Why It Doesn’t
articles.summary: The companies’ Fourth of July plans include celebrating new reactor designs coming online. But there’s still a long way to go before they deliver energy at a meaningful scale.

articles.title: Amazon updated 2023’s Fire HD 10 tablet with 4GB of RAM
articles.summary: The Fire HD 8 that launched in 2024 was the last new addition to Amazon's budget-minded tablet lineup, but the company has quietly updated the Fire HD 10 that debuted the year before. articles.title: Chevy built an all-American EV truck — why is nobody buying it?
articles.summary: The Chevy Silverado EV is a solid first draft of an EV pickup truck. Here's what could make it better.

articles.title: NASA’s Hubble Captures Crimson Cloud Sparkling with White, Blue Stars
articles.summary: Blue and white stars shine brightly against crimson gas in this image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope.

articles.title: NASA’s Hubble Spots Star-Spangled Cosmic Scene
articles.summary: More than 500,000 stars blaze red, white, and blue in this NASA Hubble image of the globular cluster Messier 3 (M3).

articles.title: All Your Favorite Gadgets Are Getting Way More Expensive … Again
articles.summary: Thanks to the AI-driven chip shortage, prices for phones, computers, and consoles are sky-high—and still climbing.

articles.title: The FDA Ruled That ZYN Pouches Are Safer Than Cigarettes. That Doesn’t Mean They’re Safe
articles.summary: ZYN nicotine pouches, the FDA found, can be advertised as a less-harmful option for adult smokers. But quitting all tobacco and nicotine products remains the best idea.

articles.title: When the Law Kills Your Electric Car Dealership
articles.summary: Dealers who invested in Polestar won’t be able to sell in the US next year after the federal government denied an authorization that would have allowed the company to avoid a Chinese tech ban.

articles.title: Politician who investigated spyware abuses had his phone hacked with Pegasus spyware
articles.summary: A government customer of NSO Group used the company's Pegasus spyware to hack into the phone of a European politician, who at the time was serving on an EU committee tasked with investigating the spyware industry.

articles.title: Last chance to apply — Startup Battlefield Australia applications close July 6
articles.summary: If you're going to apply for Startup Battlefield Australia, now is the time. Applications close July 6, and once the deadline passes, the opportunity is gone.

articles.title: The Onion’s ‘Infowars’ Parody Is Here. Alex Jones Is Going to Hate It
articles.summary: The satirical site is fighting to officially take over Infowars. In the meantime, CEO Ben Collins says the new show will mock “how fucking stupid” conspiratorial brain rot has become.

articles.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
articles.summary: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.

articles.title: IQM, Europe’s first public quantum company, admits the future of the tech is uncertain
articles.summary: IQM, a full-stack quantum company out of Finland, went public on the Nasdaq today at a valuation of about $1.9 billion.

articles.title: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
articles.summary: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app that lets users generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts.

articles.summary: A new result using NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory shows that the outer spiral arms in the Milky Way galaxy may reach wider than previously thought. articles.title: NASA Seeks Volunteers for New Yearlong Simulated Moon, Mars Mission
articles.summary: NASA is recruiting research participants for the agency’s next simulated deep space mission.
